last_completed_round.direction_name: Restore The Grouped-Row Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling Surface
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 25 gave the structural answer we needed from the auxiliary 256x128 family: the branch stayed correct, but runtime regressed to 24.690687 ms and the machine state stayed trapped at 48.23% tensor activity and 16.57% warps active. That is enough evidence to stop spending more immediate budget on this family. The best next move is therefore the strongest correctness-proven alternate surface already sitting in the frontier: the grouped-row non-PTX 128x128 sibling. It previously measured 24.178688 ms, which is inside the accepted-base noise band, while still replacing the PTX microkernel-specific control and export surface. That makes it the best round-26 recommendation: it recovers most of the round-25 loss without collapsing straight back into another same-surface PTX restore.

notes: Completed round 26/100. Continue with node_b for round 27/100. Accepted base: 20260420_235922_bf16_gemm_v1_489574e at 24.164272 ms.
